Title: Auxin-Producing Pseudomonas Recruited by Root Flavonoids Increases Rice Rhizosheath Formation through the Bacterial Histidine Kinase Under Soil Drying

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PSE14 whole-genome analysis; qRT-PCR after apigenin/luteolin treatment; cheA deletion mutant (ΔcheA); complementation (ΔcheA-c); capillary chemotaxis assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: cheA deletion markedly reduced bacterial chemotaxis toward flavonoids, potentially impairing recruitment to roots; no direct plant growth penalty separately quantified
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Bacterial genome sequencing, NCBI BioProject PRJNA914510
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression induced by apigenin and luteolin in bacterial culture
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cheA was identified in the chemotaxis pathway of PSE14. 2. Flavonoids apigenin and luteolin induced expression of cheA and promoted PSE14 chemotaxis. 3. Deletion of cheA strongly reduced chemotaxis toward flavonoids, supporting its requirement for flavonoid-mediated bacterial recruitment linked to rice rhizosheath formation under soil drying.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin promotes root hair elongation and rhizosheath formation, improving drought adaptation and water-use efficiency
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for developing microbial inoculants with strong flavonoid-responsive recruitment under drought; can be paired with host flavonoid-enhancing traits
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Histidine kinase; chemotaxis signaling protein CheA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: cheA affects recruitment/chemotaxis rather than auxin biosynthesis directly; metabolic effect is indirect through improved colonization of an auxin-producing bacterium
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid-induced recruitment of auxin-producing Pseudomonas, thereby enhancing rice rhizosheath formation under soil drying
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm; inner membrane-associated chemotaxis signaling system
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drying; apigenin treatment; luteolin treatment

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in tetraploid rice roots under soil drying; strongly elevated in OE1/OE2 transgenic roots
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression construct in pBWA(V)HS; specific promoter not reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; overexpression in diploid rice; root flavonoid measurement; 16S rRNA community profiling; colony counting of Pseudomonas
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA859542; 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing, NCBI BioProject PRJNA859640
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed under soil drying, elevated in tetraploid and overexpression lines
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCHS1 expression was higher in tetraploid rice roots under soil drying. 2. Overexpression of OsCHS1 increased root flavonoid concentration in diploid rice. 3. OsCHS1 overexpression shifted rhizosheath microbiota and increased Pseudomonadaceae abundance to levels similar to tetraploid rice, linking host flavonoid biosynthesis to microbial recruitment.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids act as root exudate signals that recruit beneficial rhizobacteria and support drought adaptation through rhizosheath formation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance root flavonoid exudation and recruit beneficial Pseudomonas for drought resilience; may be stacked with beneficial microbial inoculants
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although CHS is an enzyme in flavonoid biosynthesis, the paper uses OsCHS1 mainly as a host determinant modulating rhizosphere microbiota rather than dissecting catalytic flux to a specific nutritional end product
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases root flavonoid accumulation and enriches Pseudomonadaceae in the rhizosheath under soil drying

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/engineering target for selecting or constructing auxin-producing bioinoculants that enhance drought adaptation through rhizosheath formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes bacterial IAA production and thereby enhances root hair elongation, rhizosheath formation, and water-use efficiency in rice under soil drying
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Bacterial genome sequencing, NCBI BioProject PRJNA914510
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome analysis; iaaM deletion mutant (ΔiaaM); complementation (ΔiaaM-c); HPLC quantification of bacterial IAA; rice inoculation phenotyping under soil drying; NPA inhibition assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme in the IAM pathway by mutant analysis, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drying; auxin transport inhibition by NPA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: iaaM deletion reduced IAA output and lost growth-promoting/rhizosheath-promoting activity; no additional tradeoff quantified
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focused on bacterial auxin contribution to plant phenotype rather than detailed kinetics of the IAM pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-tryptophan to indole-3-acetamide, an intermediate in bacterial IAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. iaaM was identified in the PSE14 genome as an IAA biosynthesis gene. 2. Deleting iaaM lowered bacterial IAA production by 59.9%. 3. Inoculation with wild-type PSE14 or complemented ΔiaaM-c, but not ΔiaaM, increased rice root hair length, rhizosheath formation and water-use efficiency under soil drying.
